_comment: carson.systems — Next.js + SQLite content site. Import into Pelican (Admin > Eggs > Import). Pelican also accepts this Pterodactyl PTDL_v2 format.
name: carson.systems (Next.js content site)
author: imaigneyt@gmail.com
description: A Next.js (App Router) site with a SQLite-backed content API. Blogs, notes, and projects are managed over HTTP + the carsonctl CLI. Runs with `next start`; the DB persists in /home/container/data.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# ---- carson.systems (Next.js content site) — Pelican installer ----
cd /mnt/server

# 1. Get the code: git clone/pull if GIT_ADDRESS is set, else expect an SFTP upload.
if [ -n "${GIT_ADDRESS}" ]; then
    [ "${GIT_ADDRESS: -4}" == ".git" ] || GIT_ADDRESS="${GIT_ADDRESS}.git"
    [ -z "${GIT_BRANCH}" ] && GIT_BRANCH="main"
    if [ -d .git ]; then
        echo "Updating existing checkout on ${GIT_BRANCH}..."
        git remote set-url origin "${GIT_ADDRESS}"
        git fetch --all --prune
        git reset --hard "origin/${GIT_BRANCH}"
    else
        echo "Cloning ${GIT_ADDRESS} (${GIT_BRANCH})..."
        git clone --depth 1 --branch "${GIT_BRANCH}" "${GIT_ADDRESS}" /tmp/app
        cp -a /tmp/app/. /mnt/server/
        rm -rf /tmp/app
    fi
else
    echo "GIT_ADDRESS not set — using files already in /home/container (SFTP upload)."
fi

if [ ! -f package.json ]; then
    echo "ERROR: no package.json in the server root. Set GIT_ADDRESS or upload the project via SFTP."
    exit 1
fi

# 2. Install dependencies (dev deps included — needed for the build + seed).
npm ci || npm install

# 3. Build the production bundle.
npm run build

# 4. Seed the content DB (idempotent — skips if already populated).
npm run seed || echo "seed skipped/failed (non-fatal)"

echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "Install complete. Set CONTENT_API_TOKEN and start the server."
echo "-----------------------------------------"

variables.name: Content API token
variables.description: Bearer token that protects the write API (POST/PUT/DELETE on /api/*) and is used by carsonctl. Generate a long random value. Writes are disabled if this is empty.

variables.name: Git repository
variables.description: HTTPS URL of the repo to deploy, e.g. https://github.com/CarsonKopec/PersonalWebsite. Leave blank to deploy files you upload via SFTP instead.

variables.name: SQLite DB path
variables.description: Where the content database lives. Keep it under /home/container so it persists across restarts and reinstalls.
